rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LAMANTEA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kerr Corporation'S Motion For Leave To Conduct Destructive Testing
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Monday, September 28, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Kerr Corporation¿s motion for leave to conduct destructive testing is granted on the conditions that: 1) the testing be completed by October 22; 2) Kerr inform all other defendants and Berry & Berry that it is doing the testing and ensure that no other testing will be done by or on behalf of defendants; 3) no later than October 22 Kerr inform counsel for plaintiffs whether it will seek to introduce evidence of the testing at trial and, if so, make available the person(s) who performed the tests for deposition no later than October 27. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LAMANTEA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct Destructive Testing
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Monday, September 28, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Kerr Corporation¿s motion for leave to conduct destructive testing is granted on the conditions that: 1) the testing be completed by October 22; 2) Kerr inform all other defendants and Berry & Berry that it is doing the testing and ensure that no other testing will be done by or on behalf of defendants; 3) no later than October 22 Kerr inform counsel for plaintiffs whether it will seek to introduce evidence of the testing at trial and, if so, make available the person(s) who performed the tests for deposition no later than October 27. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Go 129 To Plaintiff, Et A.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Monday, September 28, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Raytheon Company¿s motion to compel further responses to GO 129 interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs are required to provide further responses to set one interrogatories 11- 17, 21, 23-24, 26, 32, 34, 36, 46, 47 and set two interrogatory 1. Plaintiffs¿ responses to interrogatories 18, 22, 25, 27-31, 33, 35, 37-40, 42, 44, 45 are sufficient and no further responses to these interrogatories are required except where plaintiffs stated that they would provide further responses. Defendant is awarded sanctions in the amount of $1300 (calculated as $260 oerh hour for 5 hours). Plaintiffs¿ responses to set two interrogatory one and their failure to identify any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant lacked substantial justification. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. = (220/HEK)
